title: Manehattan-class Patrol Ship
content:
The first ship class produced by Equestria for a purpose other than logistics or exploration; the Manehattan-class was created to protect against the minor pirates that operated in the fledgling Equestrian space. Slow for it's size, and suffering from underpowered weaponry; it was only marginally successful at fulfilling it's intended role prior to the start of the Great War.

At the time of the 1st Battle of Yildun Rest, the Manehattan-class was the most heavily armed and numerous ship in the Equestrian Fleet. Within the first six months of the war, 11 of the 19 Manehattan-class ships produced had been destroyed or rendered unusable. With the introduction of the far more effective [[Cloudsdale II-class Cruiser|Cloudsdale II-class]] Cruiser, the Manehattan-class was quickly moved off of the front line; the remaining ships serving as back line patrol and support vessels. Eventually, Fillydelphia-Class escort would be created to fill the role of the Manehattan-class, with all but one of the remaining ships being scrapped for parts and material.

One of the last ships of the class, the ESS Big Apple, was converted into a museum ship, moored permanently in it's namesake city.

History

Notable vessels

  • ESAV Big Apple: Launched 2419, Equus Orbital Yards. First ship of the class. Designated as museum ship in 2444.
  • ESAV Neon Lights: Launched in 2419, Equus Orbital Yards. Destroyed with all hands in 2429 during the Battle of Beta Celestine. Took down an Apleen Destroyer via ramming action.
  • ESAV/ESS Last Call: Launched in 2421, Equus Orbital Yards. Destroyed with all hands in 2430 during the Battle of Ssal Domi. Crew posthumously consecrated as Sarden martyrs.
